---
title: "GCP Data Processing Template Repos"
---

To support consistent, reproducible workflows across projects, SWFSC CoastWatch maintains two GitHub template repositories. These templates provide a standardized starting point for new data processing projects deployed on Google Cloud Platform.

## Template Repositories

### Cloud Run Template

**Repository:** [erd-coastwatch/cloud-run-template](https://github.com/erd-coastwatch/cloud-run-template)

Use this template for containerized batch processing jobs deployed on **Google Cloud Run**. It follows the same structure used by the VIIRS NetPP workflow and is designed for jobs that run on a schedule or on demand, process data, and write results to GCS.

```text
cloud-run-template/
├── config/
│   ├── config.yml          # Runtime settings (sensors, GCS paths, API keys)
│   └── requirements.txt    # Python dependencies
├── deploy_job.sh           # Build, push, and deploy the Cloud Run job
├── Dockerfile              # Container definition
├── entrypoint.sh           # Job mode selector
├── scripts/                # Processing and orchestration logic
└── templates/              # CDL or other output templates
```

### Dataproc / VM Template

**Repository:** [erd-coastwatch/vm-dataproc-template](https://github.com/erd-coastwatch/vm-dataproc-template)

Use this template for interactive or long-running workflows that run directly on a **Dataproc or ERDDAP VM**. It is structured for projects that combine notebooks, scripts, and reusable Python modules, with access to GCS-mounted data.

```text
vm-dataproc-template/
├── README.md               # Project overview and setup instructions
├── environment.yml         # Conda environment
├── config.yml              # Configuration file
├── LICENSE
├── notebooks/              # Jupyter notebooks for exploration and analysis
├── scripts/                # Shell and Python workflow scripts
├── src/                    # Reusable Python modules and helpers
├── data/                   # Intermediate or in-process data
├── templates/              # CDL or CSV template files
├── resources/              # Misc reference files used during processing
├── logs/                   # Runtime logs
├── docs/                   # Documentation (Quarto or Sphinx)
└── tests/                  # Unit tests
```

GCS mount points follow the convention:

```text
/mnt/gcs/data/<project>/          # Large input data
/mnt/gcs/erddap_data_pw/<project>/ # ERDDAP-served output data
```

## How to Use a Template

1. **Go to the template repo** on GitHub and click **"Use this template" → "Create a new repository"**.
2. **Name your repo** following the project naming convention (e.g. `viirs-netpp`, `seaice-sst`).
3. **Clone your new repo** to your local machine or VM:

```{.bash}
git clone https://github.com/erd-coastwatch/YOUR_NEW_REPO.git
cd YOUR_NEW_REPO
```

4. **Fill in `config.yml`** with your project-specific settings - sensor names, GCS bucket paths, date ranges, and any API parameters.
5. **Add your scripts and notebooks** to the appropriate directories.
6. For Cloud Run projects, **build and deploy** using `deploy_job.sh` once your Dockerfile and scripts are ready.

::: {.callout-note}
These templates are intentionally minimal - they provide structure, not implementation. Each project will fill in the logic specific to its data source and processing requirements.
:::
